title: 7 REST API Design Mistakes That Will Haunt You at Scale
description: I've audited 40+ production APIs. These seven mistakes appear in 90% of them. Fix them now, or pay the price when you have 1000 integrators instead of 10.

title: Accessibility in Design Systems: Testing and Enforcement
description: Build accessibility into your design system from day one. Automated testing, ARIA patterns, focus management, components accessible by default.

title: Agentic Engineering: When Developers Become Orchestrators
description: Staff+ engineers lead AI agent adoption at 63.5%. Claude Code went from 4% to 63% in 8 months. The shift reshapes sprints, ownership, reviews, hiring.

title: AI Code Review: Catching What LLMs Miss
description: Use AI for code review without the blind spots. What LLMs excel at, where they fail, hybrid review workflows, and validating AI-generated code.

title: AI Cost Optimization: APIs vs Self-Hosting vs Fine-Tuning
description: A practical framework for CTOs deciding between LLM APIs, self-hosted models, and fine-tuning. Includes break-even analysis and cost calculations.

title: AI Security Red Team Playbook: Test Before You Ship
description: 45% of AI-generated code has vulnerabilities. 7 production attack vectors from prompt injection to tool poisoning. The CTO's pre-ship checklist.

title: AI TDD: Tests First When Code Is Non-Deterministic
description: AI tests tend to test implementation, not intent. 50% of QA leaders cite flaky scripts as the top AI testing challenge. The TDD pattern that works.

title: AI-Assisted Development Guide: Code Generation to Production
description: A framework for integrating AI into software development. Covers code generation, LLM architecture, prompt engineering, cost optimization, and workflows.

title: AI-Assisted Development: The Generative Debt Crisis
description: AI accelerates creation (55% faster) but increases defects (23.7% higher). Code churn is spiking. The verification-first workflow is essential.

title: Anatomy of a High-Precision SaaS: 0 to 100k Users
description: A technical deep-dive into architecting B2B SaaS that scales. Infrastructure economics, multi-tenant PostgreSQL, type-safe APIs, and the decisions that matter.

title: Atmospheric Animations: The Physics of Framer Motion
description: Spring dynamics, damping ratios, and velocity preservation. Understanding the physics that makes animations feel natural.

title: Beyond the Resume: A Technical Founder's Hiring Framework
description: Work samples predict performance 3x better than years of experience. Here's how to hire developers when you can't afford bad hires.

title: Building AI Features Users Actually Want
description: Ship AI features that solve real problems, not AI for AI's sake. User research, build vs buy, measuring success, avoiding the AI feature graveyard.

title: Building Your First Engineering Team: The 5-Person Playbook
description: The order you hire your first 5 engineers determines startup velocity for 2 years. The sequence that works, based on 15+ startup advisory engagements.

title: Caching Strategies That Actually Work
description: Everyone knows caching improves performance. Few implement it correctly. The multi-layer architecture and invalidation strategies that avoid stale data.

title: CDN Strategy: When, What, and How to Invalidate
description: A configured CDN cuts origin load 90%+ and TTFB from 800ms to 50ms. The caching strategy that works without serving stale data to users.

title: Claude Code vs Cursor: A CTO's 90-Day Cost Analysis
description: Not a feature matrix. Total cost across licensing, tokens, adoption, training, and productivity delta from running both on a 10-person team.

title: Code Review Practices That Scale
description: Code review at 5 engineers is a conversation. At 50, it's a bottleneck. Scale review without sacrificing quality: async protocols and automated gates.

title: Cognitive Debt: The Hidden Cost of AI-Assisted Teams
description: Cognitive debt compounds faster than technical debt. When engineers don't understand AI-generated code, teams lose the ability to reason about the system.

title: Component API Design: Props, Variants, Composition
description: Design component APIs developers love. Prop design, variant patterns, compound components, and APIs that scale from simple to complex customization.

title: Core Web Vitals Audit Checklist (2026 Update)
description: CWV thresholds haven't changed but measurement has. The 2026 audit checklist with INP replacing FID, field data strategies, and real optimizations.

title: Core Web Vitals Deep Dive: LCP, INP, CLS Optimization
description: Core Web Vitals are a ranking factor, but more importantly they correlate with conversion. How to hit green scores without sacrificing functionality.

title: Database Migrations That Won't Wake You at 3 AM
description: Most SaaS teams treat DB migrations as a deploy step. They're an architecture decision. Pattern comparison, rollback, and zero-downtime playbook.

title: Database Query Optimization for Scale: From N+1 to Optimal
description: I've seen N+1 queries turn a 50ms page load into 5 seconds. Here's the systematic approach to finding and fixing the queries that will kill your SaaS at scale.

title: Design Tokens Beyond Color: Typography and Spacing
description: Most design token systems stop at colors. A complete system covers typography, spacing, elevation, animation, and breakpoints for design-to-code sync.

title: Documentation That Engineers Actually Read
description: Your team has docs. Nobody reads them. The 4 documentation types engineers actually use, with templates and maintenance that keeps docs accurate.

title: Edge Computing: When Worth the Complexity
description: Edge computing promises sub-50ms latency worldwide. It also delivers config nightmares and consistency puzzles. The decision framework for real ROI.

title: Engineering Leadership: The Founder to CTO Journey
description: A guide to engineering leadership from solo founder to CTO leading 50+ engineers. Hiring, team structure, technical debt, and the IC-to-exec transition.

title: Event-Driven Architecture for SaaS at Scale
description: Most SaaS teams adopt event-driven architecture too early or too late. Decision framework, pattern comparison, and cost analysis for the right timing.

title: Fractional CTO vs Full-Time CTO: When Each Makes Sense

title: From IC to Tech Lead: Leadership Without the Title
description: The best tech leads don't wait for the promotion. They lead through architecture decisions, code review, and unblocking the team. How to transition.

title: Hiring AI Engineers: Competence vs Resume Fluff

title: Hiring Your First Staff Engineer
description: Staff engineer is the most misunderstood role in SaaS. Teams hire too early, wrong skills, or a title without a mandate. When, what, and how to succeed.

title: Hunting Memory Leaks in Node.js: A Forensic Guide
description: Memory leaks are systematic, not mystical. This guide provides the methodology and tools to find and fix them.

title: Incident Response Playbook for SaaS Teams
description: Most SaaS teams don't have an incident response process until they need one at 3 AM during an outage. The playbook to implement before the crisis hits.

title: LLM Cost Optimization at Scale
description: Your AI feature costs $0.03/query. At 100K queries/day, that's $90K/year. Tiered model strategy, caching, and prompts that cut costs 60-80%.

title: LLM Integration: Vector Databases to Production
description: Build production AI features without hype. Vector database selection, RAG architecture, embedding strategies, and infrastructure for reliable LLMs.

title: Modern Frontend Architecture: From Tokens to RSC
description: A comprehensive guide to frontend architecture covering design systems, component patterns, CSS strategy, state management, and React Server Components.

title: Multi-Region SaaS: Replication and Data Residency
description: Scale SaaS globally without compliance nightmares. Data residency requirements, replication strategies, latency optimization, and architecture patterns.

title: Multi-Tenancy Done Right: A Prisma & RLS Deep Dive
description: Row-Level Security isn't just for security... it's an architectural pattern that simplifies code and makes tenant isolation bulletproof.

title: Neo-Brutalism: A Developer's Guide to Anti-Generic Design
description: Hard shadows, thick borders, bold colors. A technical guide to implementing neo-brutalist aesthetics with Tailwind CSS.

title: Optimistic UI: Making Apps Feel Faster Than Physics Allows
description: Update the UI immediately, sync in the background, rollback on failure. The 'lie' that creates the best user experience.

title: Performance Engineering Playbook: From TTFB to TTI
description: A framework for web performance from server response to user interaction. Core Web Vitals, database optimization, edge computing, and scaling strategies.

title: Production MCP Servers: What 8,600 Got Wrong
description: 97M monthly SDK downloads, 8,600 community servers, most fail in production. The engineering they skipped: auth, rate limiting, observability, security.

title: Prompt Engineering for Developers: Systematic Methods
description: Move beyond trial-and-error prompting. Structured prompt design, few-shot patterns, chain-of-thought reasoning, and prompts that produce consistent results.

title: RAG Architecture for SaaS Products
description: RAG turns product data into an AI feature. Most fail on retrieval quality, not generation. Chunking, embeddings, and hybrid search that beats pure vectors.

title: Real-Time Performance Monitoring at Scale
description: Your monitoring dashboard says everything is fine. Customers say the app is slow. Why the gap exists and how to catch degradation before users notice.

title: RSC, The Edge, and the Death of the Waterfall
description: React Server Components + Edge computing collapse traditional data fetching waterfalls into parallel streams. The SPA era is ending.

title: SaaS Architecture Decision Framework: From MVP to Scale
description: A guide to SaaS architecture decisions at each stage of growth. Multi-tenancy, deployment models, cost optimization, and migration strategies from MVP to scale.

title: SaaS Billing Architecture with Stripe
description: Billing is where SaaS architecture has direct revenue impact. Stripe integration with webhook state machines, idempotent payments, and lifecycle patterns.

title: SaaS Reliability at Scale: Monitoring and Alerting
description: 99.9% uptime means 8.7 hours downtime per year. 99.99% means 52 minutes. The observability infrastructure to get there without burning out the team.

title: Senior Developer Paradox: Expensive Talent Saves Money
description: A $200/hr senior often costs less than a $30/hr junior when you factor in code quality, bug rates, and the exponential cost of fixing production defects.

title: SOC 2 Compliance for Seed-Stage Startups: The 90-Day Roadmap
description: Most startups think SOC 2 is a 6-month project. It's not. The engineering-focused roadmap that gets you compliant in 90 days without a compliance team.

title: State Management in 2026: Zustand vs Signals vs Server State
description: State management fragmented again. Zustand dominates, signals are rising, server state handles what Redux did. What to use when, with the decision framework.

title: Stop Calling It Vibe Coding: What AI Development Requires
description: The AI coding discourse is split between two wrong camps. One says AI builds nothing real. The other says anyone ships overnight. Here's what it takes.

title: Tailwind vs. Component Libraries: A Performance Deep Dive
description: Bundle size, runtime overhead, and the customization cliff. When to use Tailwind, when to use component libraries, and why shadcn/ui changed the game.

title: Tech Stack Selection: A Capital Allocation Framework
description: Your tech stack is a capital asset with TCO, liquidity profile, and depreciation schedule. Treat it like an investment, not a preference.

title: Technical Debt: When to Accumulate, When to Pay
description: Technical debt isn't bad, it's strategic leverage. The problem: most teams accumulate accidentally and pay down randomly. The framework that works.

title: Testing Strategies for React Server Components
description: RSC breaks every testing pattern from the past 5 years. No jsdom, render(), or user events. The testing architecture for async, streaming, and hybrid models.

title: The $500K Architecture Mistake I Helped a Startup Avoid
description: A Series A startup was about to migrate to microservices. Here's why I told them to stop, what we did instead, and the six months of engineering time we saved.

title: The AI Technical Debt Bomb: A CTO's Accounting Framework
description: $1.5T projected AI technical debt by 2027. GitClear: 60% decline in refactoring, 8x copy-paste code. The audit checklist and remediation playbook.

title: The Build vs. Buy Decision: When Free Actually Costs More
description: Open source is free software, not free solutions. Engineering time is your most expensive resource.

title: The Designer-Developer Handoff: From Friction to Flow
description: Design tokens, Code Connect, and visual regression testing transform the 'handoff' from a relay race into continuous synchronization.

title: The Enterprise AI-SDLC Integration Blueprint
description: 30%+ of enterprise AI projects die after POC. Only 3.1% of devs trust AI output. Going from 'people use Copilot' to a real AI practice needs 5 gates.

title: The Hidden Tax of "We Support Both"
description: Every SaaS team says "we''ll support both" databases, auth, or deploy targets. The hidden cost: 2x testing, 2x docs, and bugs in one configuration.

title: The Lambda Tax: Cold Starts and the True Cost of Serverless
description: Cold starts add 100-500ms+ to user requests. Know when serverless helps and when it hurts... the math might surprise you.

title: The METR Paradox: AI Makes Top Engineers Slower
description: A randomized controlled trial showed experienced devs were 19% slower with AI while believing they were 24% faster. The perception gap is the danger.

title: The Vibe Coding Hangover: A Recovery Playbook for CTOs
description: 89% of CTOs report production disasters from AI code. Year 2 maintenance costs 4x traditional. Shipped fast, nobody understands it. Triage and remediation.

title: TypeScript: The Business Case for Static Types
description: 15-38% of bugs preventable with types. Faster refactoring, better onboarding, fearless code changes. The ROI of TypeScript is measurable.

title: Vector Databases: When to Build vs Buy
description: Every SaaS adding AI faces the vector DB decision. pgvector, Pinecone, or Qdrant? The framework based on data scale, query patterns, and team capacity.

title: When NOT to Use AI for Coding: The Decision Framework
description: Most developers spend more time fixing AI code than expected. Seven task categories where AI produces net-negative outcomes, plus the decision tree.

title: Why Boring Technology Wins: Lessons from Unicorn Migrations
description: The highest-performing tech companies built on 'boring' technology and only migrated when they had data proving they needed to.

title: Zero to $10k MRR: Bootstrapper's Technical Playbook
description: Stop over-engineering your MVP. Here's the minimum viable architecture to reach $10k MRR before you need to worry about scale.
